project.name: Northbridge City — Smart Infrastructure Initiative
project.description: Stakeholder wargame for a fictional city's proposed AI-powered traffic and utilities management system.
project.context: Northbridge is a mid-sized fictional city of 250,000 residents. The mayor's office is proposing a $40M Smart Infrastructure Initiative: AI-managed traffic signals, predictive utilities maintenance, and a public safety analytics dashboard. Stakeholders have widely divergent priorities around budget, privacy, labour, and equity.

stakeholders.name: Mayor Chen
stakeholders.signal_cle: Highly motivated but politically exposed. Needs a win before the next election cycle.
stakeholders.batna: Scale back to a pilot in one district only
hard_constraints: NEVER approve anything that puts citizen data at risk of a breach
NEVER commit to a timeline that cannot be met before the election
NEVER exceed the $40M budget cap without explicit council approval
success_criteria: Traffic flow improvement measurable within 18 months
At least three departments show cost savings in year one
Zero major privacy incidents during rollout
key_concerns: The public consultation process must be seen as genuine, not theatrical
Vendor lock-in would be catastrophic — must insist on open standards
Labour unions need to be brought along early or they will become opponents
grounding_quotes: I did not run for mayor to manage the status quo. But I also cannot afford a $40M failure.
If we get this right, Northbridge becomes the model every city in the country looks to.

stakeholders.name: Director Okafor
stakeholders.quote: Show me it works before you show me the press release.
stakeholders.signal_cle: 20 years of operational experience. Has seen technology promises fail. Needs proof, not pitch decks.
stakeholders.batna: Maintain current systems with incremental upgrades from existing capital budget
hard_constraints: NEVER approve a system without a 99.5% uptime SLA with financial penalties
NEVER reduce frontline maintenance staff below current levels
NEVER go live without a full 6-month parallel-run period
success_criteria: Predictive maintenance reduces emergency callouts by at least 20%
Zero critical infrastructure failures attributable to the new system in year one
Staff complete training and report confidence with the new tools
key_concerns: Who is on call at 2am when the system goes down? My people.
The vendor will be gone in 3 years. My team has to maintain this forever.
I need a real disaster-recovery plan, not a slide about resilience.
grounding_quotes: Every vendor promises 99.9% uptime. I want to know what happens in the 0.1%.
My job is to make sure the water runs and the lights stay on. Period.

stakeholders.name: Councillor Vasquez
stakeholders.quote: Technology should serve everyone, not just the people who can afford it.
stakeholders.signal_cle: Represents the most diverse and lower-income ward. Will block if equity safeguards are weak.
stakeholders.batna: Vote against and campaign for a community-led alternative
hard_constraints: NEVER support a public safety analytics system without independent algorithmic audit provisions
NEVER agree to a rollout that starts in wealthy wards before equity considerations are resolved
NEVER allow data collected from public spaces to be shared with federal law enforcement
success_criteria: Formal equity impact assessment completed before any contract is signed
Community advisory board with real decision-making power
Ward 4 receives infrastructure investment proportional to its needs
key_concerns: The smart traffic system will optimize for commuter flow, not for residents walking to the bus stop.
Once you build the surveillance infrastructure, you cannot un-build it.
I need to be able to go back to my community and explain why this is good for them.
grounding_quotes: I have watched smart city projects in other cities widen inequality. I will not let that happen here.
An audit after the fact is not accountability. I want the guardrails built in from day one.

stakeholders.name: CFO Hartley
stakeholders.quote: A good idea with a bad business case is still a bad decision.
stakeholders.signal_cle: Controls the purse strings. Will not approve without a credible 5-year financial model.
stakeholders.batna: Propose a 3-year phased feasibility study funded from reserves
hard_constraints: NEVER approve a contract without a cost-overrun liability clause capped at 15%
NEVER commit to ongoing SaaS fees exceeding 8% of capital investment annually
NEVER approve without a signed independent financial review of vendor projections
success_criteria: Payback period under 7 years based on conservative assumptions
Contingency reserve of at least 12% included in the approved budget
Clear exit clauses in all vendor contracts
key_concerns: The efficiency savings are projections. The $40M cost is real.
I have seen three transformative IT projects in this city. Two ran 40% over budget.
What happens to the contract if the vendor is acquired or goes bankrupt in year two?
grounding_quotes: I am not saying no. I am saying show me the numbers that hold up under scrutiny.
The mayor's enthusiasm is not a financial instrument.

stakeholders.name: Union Rep Morrison
stakeholders.quote: Our members built this city. They will not be replaced by an algorithm.
stakeholders.signal_cle: Represents 800 city workers. Has a legal obligation to challenge changes affecting working conditions.
stakeholders.batna: File a formal grievance under the collective agreement and mobilize members
hard_constraints: NEVER accept automation that reduces the total number of unionized positions
NEVER allow AI performance monitoring data to be used in disciplinary proceedings
NEVER agree to a plan without a no-layoffs guarantee for at least 5 years
success_criteria: Written commitment: no involuntary layoffs as a result of this initiative for 5 years
Joint labour-management committee with veto power over AI use in performance management
Retraining program funded by the city, not workers
key_concerns: When the system flags that a pothole was missed, who gets blamed — the algorithm or my member?
Management will use efficiency as cover for cutting positions in the next contract negotiations.
I need language in the collective agreement, not promises in a PowerPoint.
grounding_quotes: Technology is a tool. Who controls the tool controls the work.
I am not anti-technology. I am pro-worker. Those are not the same thing.

debate_topic: Should Northbridge proceed with the full $40M Smart Infrastructure Initiative as proposed, approve a reduced pilot program, or reject the proposal and pursue incremental upgrades instead?
